objective: Architect sign-off on the active_mode resolver + determine_surface rewrite fixing bug-1 (sticky explore surface).
key_changes: Surface selection now derives from declared config.mode via shared active_mode(root, ModeChoice)->Mode resolver in core::config, not an incidental explore.json artifact.
Resolver is shared between serve and doctor, preventing divergence between the two surface-selection paths.
Precedence preserved and explicit: ForceStandard->Mcp, ForceExplore->McpLlm, None->GroveConfig::load.mode (Mcp on failure); --standard wins over --explore.
determine_surface branches on active_mode != Mode::McpLlm -> Standard as explicit catch-all; future Mode variants route safely to Standard.
Health-gated fallback (mcp-llm + unhealthy provider -> standard) preserved unchanged; health_probe path intact.
Change contained to core/src/config.rs and cli/src/mcp.rs; no cross-cutting concerns; double GroveConfig::load is benign and documented.
268 tests pass, clippy -D warnings clean, release build clean; bug1 integration test asserts exactly 7 tools and absence of 'explore' tool.
Deployment: no migrations, no schema changes, no topology impact; material change requiring a version bump at release.
Follow-up: AM-1 fixture JSON has stray trailing quote (test passes via ForceStandard short-circuit) — cleanup recommended.
Follow-up: active_mode None path is not side-effect free (load may migrate + write config.json); a doc comment would aid future readers.
